dimensions.note: No secrets handled or stored. Uses ambient AWS credential chain via RDSClient default config; region is non-secret config. Nothing vaulted, nothing sensitive logged.

info on start, fetched-count, dropped-non-RDS, match count, and write totals; debug retry events via retryDeps; warn on a cluster missing an identifier. Structured placeholders throughout; no secrets logged.

Selector compiled and region resolved before any AWS call, so bad config fails closed with no API spend; evaluateSelector throws a typed message on non-boolean predicates. All throws precede writeResource. Region resolution names every adjustable source.

tests/ holds CEL, smoke (fixture replay through the RdsApi facade), and unit suites plus fixtures (aurora 2/3-node, multi-AZ cluster, shared-endpoint mixed engines, empty region). Colocated test pins manifest/version sync and the upgrade-chain invariant. Failure paths (no region, non-boolean selector) covered.

Read-only list with deterministic spec-prefixed instance keys (clusterKey/instanceKey), so re-runs overwrite the same instances rather than duplicating. withRetry handles throttling with full-jitter backoff; no created cloud resources to orphan.

DescribeDBClusters and DescribeDBInstances both paginated to exhaustion via Marker; instances narrowed server-side by db-cluster-id filter and chunked at MAX_CLUSTER_IDS_PER_FILTER (200) to avoid oversized requests. AWS shapes treated as all-optional and defaulted before use. Throttling retried.
RDSClient is created once per run and used only for read calls; no file handles, sockets, or temp files. No created resources to track for cleanup.

README selector examples use generic field/value placeholders; fixtures use synthetic cluster/instance identifiers. The 2026.06.08.1 release was an account-id placeholder scrub (per the upgrades note). No real IPs, hostnames, or account ids.

ClusterSchema and InstanceSchema are plain z.object (no .passthrough()); tags use z.record(string,string). Role is a z.enum. Public resource shapes are declared as explicit interfaces (not z.infer) so deno doc --lint stays clean.
Both cluster and instance specs declare lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10.

Inventory/discovery model, not a CRUD lifecycle. list_clusters is a factory that re-derives cluster + member resources each run; there is no AWS resource this model owns to create/update/delete. Re-running list_clusters is the drift-detection mechanism, so a separate sync method is unnecessary.
list_clusters performs only read-only Describe calls; there is no mutating action a policy/live preflight label would gate.

Mechanical check: cluster keys are 'cluster-<id>' and instance keys are 'instance-<clusterId>--<instanceId>', so the two specs cannot collide even when an AWS cluster and instance share a bare name. Each spec is written by only the one method, with a consistent schema shape.
Single-pass factory writer; it never reads back previously stored swamp state (no update/delete/sync method), so the readResource-vs-dataRepository concern does not arise.

model.upgrades carries a no-op chain whose final toVersion equals model.version (2026.06.08.1); the colocated test asserts that invariant. globalArguments (region, selector) are unchanged across the chain, so the identity upgradeAttributes are correct.
